import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")
    experiment_data = None

if experiment_data is not None:
    datasets = ["colored_mnist", "colored_fashion_mnist", "colored_cifar10"]
    layers = ["conv1", "conv2", "fc1"]

    # Plot 1: Training and Validation Accuracy Curves
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4))
        for col, ds in enumerate(datasets):
            res = experiment_data["all_layers"][ds]
            axes[col].plot(res["metrics"]["train"], label="Train", alpha=0.8)
            axes[col].plot(res["metrics"]["val"], label="Val", alpha=0.8)
            axes[col].set_title(f"{ds}\nTraining & Validation Accuracy")
            axes[col].set_xlabel("Epoch")
            axes[col].set_ylabel("Accuracy (%)")
            axes[col].legend()
            axes[col].grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_training_curves.png"), dpi=150)
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating training curves plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 2: Velocity Signatures by Layer for Each Dataset
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 3, figsize=(15, 12))
        for col, ds in enumerate(datasets):
            res = experiment_data["all_layers"][ds]
            for row, layer in enumerate(layers):
                grads = res["velocity_data"][f"{layer}_grad"]
                spurious = res["layer_analysis"][layer]["spurious_neurons"]
                axes[row, col].plot(
                    grads[:, spurious].mean(axis=1),
                    label="Spurious",
                    color="red",
                    alpha=0.8,
                    linewidth=2,
                )
                axes[row, col].plot(
                    grads[:, ~spurious].mean(axis=1),
                    label="Core",
                    color="blue",
                    alpha=0.8,
                    linewidth=2,
                )
                sep = res["layer_analysis"][layer]["velocity_signature_separation"]
                axes[row, col].set_title(f"{ds}\n{layer} Velocity (sep={sep:.3f})")
                axes[row, col].legend()
                axes[row, col].grid(True, alpha=0.3)
                axes[row, col].set_xlabel("Epoch")
                axes[row, col].set_ylabel("Gradient Norm")
        plt.suptitle(
            "Velocity Signatures: Spurious (Red) vs Core (Blue) Neurons by Layer",
            fontsize=14,
            y=1.02,
        )
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_velocity_signatures_by_layer.png"),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating velocity signatures plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 3: Layer Comparison Bar Charts
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 5))
        for i, ds in enumerate(datasets):
            res = experiment_data["all_layers"][ds]
            seps = [
                res["layer_analysis"][l]["velocity_signature_separation"]
                for l in layers
            ]
            improvements = [
                res["inference"][f"lvs_{l}"]["worst"] - res["inference"]["erm"]["worst"]
                for l in layers
            ]
            x = np.arange(len(layers))
            width = 0.35
            ax1 = axes[i]
            ax2 = ax1.twinx()
            ax1.bar(
                x - width / 2,
                seps,
                width,
                label="Separation",
                color="steelblue",
                alpha=0.7,
            )
            ax2.bar(
                x + width / 2,
                improvements,
                width,
                label="Improvement (%)",
                color="coral",
                alpha=0.7,
            )
            ax1.set_xlabel("Layer")
            ax1.set_ylabel("Velocity Separation", color="steelblue")
            ax2.set_ylabel("Worst-Group Improvement (%)", color="coral")
            ax1.set_xticks(x)
            ax1.set_xticklabels(layers)
            ax1.set_title(f"{ds}\nLeft axis: Separation, Right axis: Improvement")
            ax1.legend(loc="upper left")
            ax2.legend(loc="upper right")
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_layer_effectiveness_comparison.png"),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating layer comparison plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 4: ERM vs LVS Worst-Group Accuracy Summary
    try:
        fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))
        x = np.arange(len(datasets))
        width = 0.15
        for i, ds in enumerate(datasets):
            res = experiment_data["all_layers"][ds]
            erm_worst = res["inference"]["erm"]["worst"]
            ax.bar(
                x[i] - 1.5 * width,
                erm_worst,
                width,
                color="gray",
                alpha=0.8,
                label="ERM" if i == 0 else "",
            )
            colors = ["green", "orange", "purple"]
            for j, layer in enumerate(layers):
                lvs_worst = res["inference"][f"lvs_{layer}"]["worst"]
                ax.bar(
                    x[i] + (j - 0.5) * width,
                    lvs_worst,
                    width,
                    color=colors[j],
                    alpha=0.8,
                    label=f"LVS-{layer}" if i == 0 else "",
                )
        ax.set_xlabel("Dataset")
        ax.set_ylabel("Worst-Group Accuracy (%)")
        ax.set_title(
            "Ablation Study: ERM vs Layer-Specific LVS Reweighting\nWorst-Group Accuracy Comparison"
        )
        ax.set_xticks(x)
        ax.set_xticklabels(datasets, rotation=15)
        ax.legend()
        ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis="y")
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_erm_vs_lvs_worst_group.png"), dpi=150
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating ERM vs LVS plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 5: Worst-Group Accuracy During Training
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4))
        for col, ds in enumerate(datasets):
            res = experiment_data["all_layers"][ds]
            axes[col].plot(res["worst_group"], color="darkred", linewidth=2)
            axes[col].set_title(f"{ds}\nWorst-Group Accuracy During Training")
            axes[col].set_xlabel("Epoch")
            axes[col].set_ylabel("Worst-Group Accuracy (%)")
            axes[col].grid(True, alpha=0.3)
        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_worst_group_training.png"), dpi=150
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating worst-group training plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    print("All plots saved successfully to working directory.")
plot_plan:
Looking at the experiment code, I need to create visualizations for an ablation study comparing velocity tracking across different CNN layers (conv1, conv2, fc1) for three datasets (colored_mnist, colored_fashion_mnist, colored_cifar10). The experiment data contains training metrics, velocity data for each layer, layer analysis with separation metrics, and inference results comparing ERM baseline with layer-specific LVS reweighting.

I'll create plots showing: 1) Training/validation accuracy curves for each dataset, 2) Velocity signatures comparing spurious vs core neurons across layers, 3) Bar charts comparing layer effectiveness (separation vs worst-group improvement), 4) A summary comparison of ERM vs LVS performance across datasets and layers.

plot_analyses.analysis:
ERM vs layer-specific LVS reweighting (worst-group accuracy bar chart): Across all three datasets, layer-specific LVS reweighting does not consistently improve worst-group accuracy over ERM, and can substantially degrade it depending on the layer chosen.
- colored_mnist: ERM is ~86–87%. LVS-conv1 drops a few points (~82–83%). LVS-conv2 drops more (~75–76%). LVS-fc1 is roughly on par with ERM (~86%).
- colored_fashion_mnist: ERM is ~95–96%. LVS-conv1 is lower (~92–93%). LVS-conv2 is close to ERM (~95%). LVS-fc1 is essentially tied with ERM (~95–96%).
- colored_cifar10: ERM is ~43–44%. LVS-conv2 collapses (~14%). LVS-fc1 is slightly higher than ERM (~45%). LVS-conv1 appears near-zero/very low (bar is not visible or extremely small), indicating a severe failure mode.
Implication: naive layer-local reweighting can be harmful, especially in early conv layers, and the only layer that is consistently safe here is fc1 (and sometimes conv2 on fashion). This suggests spurious-feature detectors may be more cleanly separable/controllable in later layers, or that early-layer reweighting disrupts general feature extraction.

Training vs validation accuracy curves: All datasets reach near-perfect training accuracy quickly (~99–100%), while validation accuracy is much lower and noisier, especially for colored_cifar10.
- colored_mnist: train saturates ~100% by ~5–10 epochs; val rises from mid-70s to low-90s with noticeable oscillations.
- colored_fashion_mnist: train saturates ~100% quickly; val stabilizes high (~96–98%) with moderate noise.
- colored_cifar10: train remains very high (~96→99%); val is much lower (~68→85%) and highly volatile.
Implication: strong overfitting / reliance on shortcut features is plausible, particularly for colored_cifar10 where the generalization gap and variance are largest. This supports the motivation for spurious-feature diagnostics, but also indicates that any intervention must be robust to training instability.

Velocity separation vs worst-group improvement by layer (dual-axis bars): Separation scores (blue) can be high even when worst-group improvement (orange) is negative, indicating that “detectability” via velocity signatures does not translate directly into beneficial reweighting with the current intervention.
- colored_mnist: separation is ~2.0 (conv1/conv2) and ~4.2 (fc1). Improvement is negative for conv1 (~-4%) and strongly negative for conv2 (~-11%). fc1 shows ~0% improvement (near neutral).
- colored_fashion_mnist: separation ~1.65–1.85 (conv layers) and ~2.7 (fc1). Improvements are mildly negative (~-0.7% to ~-3.4%), with fc1 near 0.
- colored_cifar10: separation is very high for conv1 (~4.24) and fc1 (~4.64), moderate for conv2 (~2.0). Yet improvements are ~0 for conv1/fc1 and extremely negative for conv2 (~-30%).
Implication: (1) separation metric may be capturing a difference between sets labeled “spurious/core” but the reweighting rule may be mis-specified (wrong direction, wrong magnitude, or wrong layer coupling). (2) Early-layer interventions are particularly brittle; even with high separation, perturbing early representations can harm worst-group performance.

Per-layer velocity (gradient norm) trajectories for spurious vs core neurons: The curves show clear and layer/dataset-dependent dynamics, but they often contradict the stated hypothesis that spurious features learn faster and plateau earlier.
- conv1:
  - colored_mnist & colored_fashion_mnist: core gradients are consistently higher than spurious across epochs, with both decaying over time. This suggests core-related neurons are receiving larger updates than spurious ones in conv1.
  - colored_cifar10: core gradients are dramatically higher (~0.1→0.32 plateau) than spurious (~0.03→0.05), yielding very large separation. This looks like a scale mismatch rather than “spurious learns fast then plateaus”; core dominates throughout.
- conv2:
  - colored_mnist: spurious starts higher than core early and decays; by late epochs they converge. This is the closest to the hypothesized “fast early” spurious behavior.
  - colored_fashion_mnist: core starts higher; both decay; separation modest.
  - colored_cifar10: spurious is consistently higher than core and decays slowly; core is relatively flat and lower. Here “spurious” receives larger gradients throughout, not just early.
- fc1:
  - All datasets: core gradients start higher and decay; spurious gradients are near-zero very early and remain near-zero. This yields high separation but indicates that the identified “spurious” fc1 neurons are effectively inactive/low-update.
Implication: The operational definition of “spurious vs core neurons” used for these plots may not align with the conceptual notion of spurious features being simpler/faster. In particular, fc1 shows “spurious” neurons with near-zero gradients, which would make reweighting them largely irrelevant (explaining near-zero improvement). For cifar10 conv1, the huge core gradient suggests the partition might be inverted or dominated by scaling effects across channels.

Worst-group accuracy during training: Worst-group accuracy is highly non-monotonic and noisy, especially on colored_cifar10.
- colored_mnist: rises quickly from ~30% to ~70–85% with large oscillations; ends ~82–83%.
- colored_fashion_mnist: quickly reaches ~90–96% with dips; ends ~96%.
- colored_cifar10: starts ~19%, climbs to ~45–55% with extreme swings (drops to low-40s) and occasional peaks ~63–65%; ends ~57–58%.
Implication: Evaluating interventions at a single endpoint may be misleading; you may need best-checkpoint selection based on a proxy (or use EMA / longer averaging) to reduce variance. The volatility also suggests that per-epoch velocity estimates and plateau detection may be unstable unless smoothed or computed over windows.

Repeated separation vs improvement summary (same dual-axis bar figure appears again): Confirms the key pattern—higher separation does not imply positive worst-group gains; conv2 reweighting is consistently harmful (especially on colored_cifar10), while fc1 is neutral and sometimes slightly positive.

Composite grid repeating accuracy and per-layer velocity plots: Reinforces earlier observations.
- Accuracy: large generalization gap, especially for colored_cifar10.
- Velocity: fc1 “spurious” gradients near-zero across datasets; conv2 is the only layer where colored_mnist shows spurious>core early.
Implication: The current LVS metric (gradient norm velocity) may be more reflective of layerwise optimization scale than feature simplicity. Consider normalizing velocities within-layer (e.g., z-score per epoch, or ratio to layer median) and verifying that “spurious” neurons correspond to color-sensitive units via targeted probes (e.g., color ablation, counterfactual recoloring) before claiming the signature matches spurious correlations.

vlm_feedback_summary: Plots show that layer-specific LVS-based reweighting is brittle: early-layer (conv1/conv2) reweighting often reduces worst-group accuracy, with catastrophic failures on colored_cifar10 (conv2 ~-30% and conv1 near-zero). fc1 reweighting is the only consistently non-harmful choice and is roughly neutral/slightly positive. Velocity separation scores can be high (especially cifar10 conv1/fc1) but do not correlate with worst-group improvement, suggesting a mismatch between the separation metric and the intervention. Per-layer gradient-norm trajectories frequently show core gradients > spurious (conv1, fc1), contradicting the hypothesized ‘spurious learns fast then plateaus’ pattern; fc1 spurious gradients are near-zero, explaining minimal gains from reweighting. Worst-group accuracy during training is highly noisy, particularly on cifar10, implying that smoothing/windowing and robust checkpointing are important for reliable LVS estimates and ablation conclusions.

import os
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")


def create_colored_mnist(n_samples=10000, spurious_corr=0.9):
    from torchvision import datasets

    mnist = datasets.MNIST(root="./data", train=True, download=True)
    images = mnist.data.numpy()[:n_samples]
    labels = mnist.targets.numpy()[:n_samples]
    binary_labels = (labels >= 5).astype(np.int64)
    colored_images = np.zeros((n_samples, 3, 28, 28), dtype=np.float32)
    spurious_labels = np.zeros(n_samples, dtype=np.int64)
    for i in range(n_samples):
        img = images[i].astype(np.float32) / 255.0
        aligned = np.random.random() < spurious_corr
        color_idx = binary_labels[i] if aligned else 1 - binary_labels[i]
        spurious_labels[i] = color_idx
        colored_images[i, color_idx] = img
    return colored_images, binary_labels, spurious_labels


def create_colored_fashion_mnist(n_samples=10000, spurious_corr=0.9):
    from datasets import load_dataset

    dataset = load_dataset("fashion_mnist", split="train")
    images = np.array([np.array(img) for img in dataset["image"][:n_samples]])
    labels = np.array(dataset["label"][:n_samples])
    tops = [0, 2, 3, 4, 6]
    binary_labels = np.array([0 if l in tops else 1 for l in labels], dtype=np.int64)
    colored_images = np.zeros((n_samples, 3, 28, 28), dtype=np.float32)
    spurious_labels = np.zeros(n_samples, dtype=np.int64)
    for i in range(n_samples):
        img = images[i].astype(np.float32) / 255.0
        aligned = np.random.random() < spurious_corr
        color_idx = binary_labels[i] if aligned else 1 - binary_labels[i]
        spurious_labels[i] = color_idx
        colored_images[i, 0 if color_idx == 0 else 2] = img
    return colored_images, binary_labels, spurious_labels


def create_colored_cifar10(n_samples=10000, spurious_corr=0.9):
    from datasets import load_dataset

    dataset = load_dataset("cifar10", split="train")
    animals = [2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
    binary_labels = np.array(
        [
            0 if dataset[i]["label"] in animals else 1
            for i in range(min(n_samples, len(dataset)))
        ],
        dtype=np.int64,
    )
    colored_images = np.zeros((len(binary_labels), 3, 32, 32), dtype=np.float32)
    spurious_labels = np.zeros(len(binary_labels), dtype=np.int64)
    for idx in range(len(binary_labels)):
        img = np.array(dataset[idx]["img"]).astype(np.float32) / 255.0
        img = img.transpose(2, 0, 1)
        aligned = np.random.random() < spurious_corr
        color_idx = binary_labels[idx] if aligned else 1 - binary_labels[idx]
        spurious_labels[idx] = color_idx
        colored_images[idx] = img
        bw = 4
        channel = 0 if color_idx == 0 else 2
        colored_images[idx, channel, :bw, :] = 1.0
        colored_images[idx, channel, -bw:, :] = 1.0
        colored_images[idx, channel, :, :bw] = 1.0
        colored_images[idx, channel, :, -bw:] = 1.0
    return colored_images, binary_labels, spurious_labels


class ImprovedCNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size=28, dropout_rate=0.3, n_hidden=128):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(3, 32, 3, padding=1)
        self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(32)
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1)
        self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(64)
        self.pool = nn.MaxPool2d(2, 2)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout_rate)
        self.relu = nn.ReLU()
        final_size = input_size // 4
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(64 * final_size * final_size, n_hidden)
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(n_hidden, 2)
        self.n_hidden = n_hidden

    def forward(self, x, return_features=False, neuron_weights=None):
        x = self.pool(self.relu(self.bn1(self.conv1(x))))
        x = self.pool(self.relu(self.bn2(self.conv2(x))))
        x = x.view(x.size(0), -1)
        features = self.relu(self.fc1(x))
        if neuron_weights is not None:
            features = features * neuron_weights.to(features.device)
        x = self.dropout(features)
        x = self.fc2(x)
        return (x, features) if return_features else x


def cohens_d(group1, group2):
    n1, n2 = len(group1), len(group2)
    if n1 < 2 or n2 < 2:
        return 0.0
    pooled_std = np.sqrt(
        ((n1 - 1) * np.var(group1) + (n2 - 1) * np.var(group2)) / (n1 + n2 - 2)
    )
    return (np.mean(group1) - np.mean(group2)) / (pooled_std + 1e-8)


def cluster_neurons_by_velocity(grad_history, n_clusters=2):
    n_epochs, n_neurons = grad_history.shape
    features = np.zeros((n_neurons, 4))
    for i in range(n_neurons):
        features[i, 0] = grad_history[:5, i].mean()
        features[i, 1] = grad_history[-10:, i].mean()
        decay_rate = (features[i, 0] - features[i, 1]) / (features[i, 0] + 1e-8)
        features[i, 2] = decay_rate
        threshold = 0.1 * grad_history[:, i].max()
        plateau_idx = np.where(grad_history[:, i] < threshold)[0]
        features[i, 3] = plateau_idx[0] if len(plateau_idx) > 0 else n_epochs
    features = (features - features.mean(axis=0)) / (features.std(axis=0) + 1e-8)
    kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=n_clusters, random_state=42, n_init=10)
    labels = kmeans.fit_predict(features)
    cluster_plateau_means = [features[labels == c, 3].mean() for c in range(n_clusters)]
    spurious_cluster = np.argmin(cluster_plateau_means)
    spurious_neurons = labels == spurious_cluster
    return spurious_neurons, ~spurious_neurons, features[:, 3]


def evaluate_with_groups(model, loader, y_true, s_true, neuron_weights=None):
    model.eval()
    all_preds = []
    with torch.no_grad():
        for batch_x, _ in loader:
            batch_x = batch_x.to(device)
            outputs = model(batch_x, neuron_weights=neuron_weights)
            _, preds = outputs.max(1)
            all_preds.extend(preds.cpu().numpy())
    all_preds = np.array(all_preds)
    groups = {}
    for y in [0, 1]:
        for s in [0, 1]:
            mask = (y_true == y) & (s_true == s)
            if mask.sum() > 0:
                groups[(y, s)] = (all_preds[mask] == y_true[mask]).mean() * 100
    avg_acc = (all_preds == y_true).mean() * 100
    worst_group = min(groups.values()) if groups else 0
    return avg_acc, worst_group, groups


def train_with_velocity_tracking(
    config, train_loader, test_loader, test_y, test_s, input_size=28
):
    torch.manual_seed(config.get("seed", 42))
    np.random.seed(config.get("seed", 42))
    model = ImprovedCNN(input_size=input_size, dropout_rate=config["dropout"]).to(
        device
    )
    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    optimizer = optim.AdamW(
        model.parameters(), lr=config["lr"], weight_decay=config["weight_decay"]
    )
    scheduler = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
        optimizer, T_max=config["n_epochs"]
    )

    velocity_data = {"fc1_grad": []}
    results = {
        "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
        "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
        "worst_group": [],
    }
    best_val_loss, patience_counter, best_model_state = float("inf"), 0, None

    for epoch in range(config["n_epochs"]):
        model.train()
        train_loss, correct, total = 0.0, 0, 0
        epoch_grads = []

        for batch_x, batch_y in train_loader:
            batch_x, batch_y = batch_x.to(device), batch_y.to(device)
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            outputs = model(batch_x)
            loss = criterion(outputs, batch_y)
            loss.backward()
            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
            epoch_grads.append(model.fc1.weight.grad.norm(dim=1).cpu().numpy())
            optimizer.step()
            train_loss += loss.item()
            _, predicted = outputs.max(1)
            total += batch_y.size(0)
            correct += predicted.eq(batch_y).sum().item()

        scheduler.step()
        velocity_data["fc1_grad"].append(np.mean(epoch_grads, axis=0))
        train_acc = 100.0 * correct / total
        avg_train_loss = train_loss / len(train_loader)

        model.eval()
        val_loss, correct, total = 0.0, 0, 0
        with torch.no_grad():
            for batch_x, batch_y in test_loader:
                batch_x, batch_y = batch_x.to(device), batch_y.to(device)
                outputs = model(batch_x)
                loss = criterion(outputs, batch_y)
                val_loss += loss.item()
                _, predicted = outputs.max(1)
                total += batch_y.size(0)
                correct += predicted.eq(batch_y).sum().item()

        val_acc = 100.0 * correct / total
        avg_val_loss = val_loss / len(test_loader)
        avg_acc, worst_group, _ = evaluate_with_groups(
            model, test_loader, test_y, test_s
        )

        results["losses"]["train"].append(avg_train_loss)
        results["losses"]["val"].append(avg_val_loss)
        results["metrics"]["train"].append(train_acc)
        results["metrics"]["val"].append(val_acc)
        results["worst_group"].append(worst_group)

        if avg_val_loss < best_val_loss:
            best_val_loss = avg_val_loss
            best_model_state = {
                k: v.cpu().clone() for k, v in model.state_dict().items()
            }
            patience_counter = 0
        else:
            patience_counter += 1

        if patience_counter >= config["patience"]:
            break

    if best_model_state:
        model.load_state_dict({k: v.to(device) for k, v in best_model_state.items()})

    fc1_grads = np.array(velocity_data["fc1_grad"])
    spurious_neurons, core_neurons, plateau_epochs = cluster_neurons_by_velocity(
        fc1_grads
    )

    separation_metrics = {
        "plateau_epoch": abs(
            cohens_d(plateau_epochs[spurious_neurons], plateau_epochs[core_neurons])
        ),
        "initial_velocity": abs(
            cohens_d(
                fc1_grads[:5].mean(axis=0)[spurious_neurons],
                fc1_grads[:5].mean(axis=0)[core_neurons],
            )
        ),
        "final_velocity": abs(
            cohens_d(
                fc1_grads[-10:].mean(axis=0)[spurious_neurons],
                fc1_grads[-10:].mean(axis=0)[core_neurons],
            )
        ),
    }
    velocity_signature_separation = np.mean(list(separation_metrics.values()))

    neuron_weights = torch.ones(model.n_hidden)
    neuron_weights[spurious_neurons] = config.get("reweight_factor", 0.3)

    avg_acc_erm, worst_erm, groups_erm = evaluate_with_groups(
        model, test_loader, test_y, test_s
    )
    avg_acc_lvs, worst_lvs, groups_lvs = evaluate_with_groups(
        model, test_loader, test_y, test_s, neuron_weights
    )

    results["velocity_data"] = velocity_data
    results["velocity_signatures"] = {
        "plateau_epochs": plateau_epochs.tolist(),
        "spurious_neurons": spurious_neurons.tolist(),
        "core_neurons": core_neurons.tolist(),
        "separation_metrics": separation_metrics,
        "velocity_signature_separation": velocity_signature_separation,
    }
    results["inference"] = {
        "erm": {
            "avg": avg_acc_erm,
            "worst": worst_erm,
            "groups": {str(k): v for k, v in groups_erm.items()},
        },
        "lvs": {
            "avg": avg_acc_lvs,
            "worst": worst_lvs,
            "groups": {str(k): v for k, v in groups_lvs.items()},
        },
    }
    return results


# Configuration
config = {
    "lr": 0.001,
    "batch_size": 128,
    "n_epochs": 60,
    "dropout": 0.4,
    "weight_decay": 5e-4,
    "seed": 42,
    "patience": 15,
    "reweight_factor": 0.3,
}

# Spurious correlation levels to test
correlation_levels = [0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99]

# Dataset creators
dataset_creators = {
    "colored_mnist": (create_colored_mnist, 28),
    "colored_fashion_mnist": (create_colored_fashion_mnist, 28),
    "colored_cifar10": (create_colored_cifar10, 32),
}

experiment_data = {}

for corr_level in correlation_levels:
    corr_key = f"corr_{corr_level}"
    experiment_data[corr_key] = {}
    print(f"\n{'='*60}\nSpurious Correlation Level: {corr_level}\n{'='*60}")

    for dataset_name, (creator_fn, input_size) in dataset_creators.items():
        print(f"\n--- Dataset: {dataset_name} ---")

        torch.manual_seed(42)
        np.random.seed(42)

        # Create training data with specified spurious correlation
        X_train, y_train, s_train = creator_fn(
            n_samples=10000, spurious_corr=corr_level
        )
        # Test data always uses 0.5 correlation for fair evaluation
        X_test, y_test, s_test = creator_fn(n_samples=2000, spurious_corr=0.5)

        mean_val, std_val = X_train.mean(), X_train.std() + 1e-8
        train_dataset = TensorDataset(
            torch.FloatTensor((X_train - mean_val) / std_val), torch.LongTensor(y_train)
        )
        test_dataset = TensorDataset(
            torch.FloatTensor((X_test - mean_val) / std_val), torch.LongTensor(y_test)
        )

        train_loader = DataLoader(
            train_dataset, batch_size=config["batch_size"], shuffle=True
        )
        test_loader = DataLoader(
            test_dataset, batch_size=config["batch_size"], shuffle=False
        )

        results = train_with_velocity_tracking(
            config, train_loader, test_loader, y_test, s_test, input_size
        )

        experiment_data[corr_key][dataset_name] = {
            "metrics": results["metrics"],
            "losses": results["losses"],
            "worst_group": results["worst_group"],
            "velocity_signatures": results["velocity_signatures"],
            "inference": results["inference"],
            "spurious_corr": corr_level,
        }

        print(
            f"  Separation Score: {results['velocity_signatures']['velocity_signature_separation']:.4f}"
        )
        print(
            f"  ERM: avg={results['inference']['erm']['avg']:.1f}%, worst={results['inference']['erm']['worst']:.1f}%"
        )
        print(
            f"  LVS: avg={results['inference']['lvs']['avg']:.1f}%, worst={results['inference']['lvs']['worst']:.1f}%"
        )
        improvement = (
            results["inference"]["lvs"]["worst"] - results["inference"]["erm"]["worst"]
        )
        print(f"  LVS Improvement: {improvement:+.1f}%")

# Summary
print("\n" + "=" * 80)
print("ABLATION STUDY SUMMARY: Spurious Correlation Strength Sensitivity")
print("=" * 80)

summary_data = {"correlation_levels": correlation_levels}
for dataset_name in dataset_creators.keys():
    summary_data[dataset_name] = {
        "separation_scores": [],
        "erm_worst": [],
        "lvs_worst": [],
        "improvements": [],
    }
    print(f"\n{dataset_name}:")
    print(
        f"{'Corr':<8} {'Separation':<12} {'ERM Worst':<12} {'LVS Worst':<12} {'Improvement':<12}"
    )
    print("-" * 56)
    for corr_level in correlation_levels:
        corr_key = f"corr_{corr_level}"
        res = experiment_data[corr_key][dataset_name]
        sep = res["velocity_signatures"]["velocity_signature_separation"]
        erm_worst = res["inference"]["erm"]["worst"]
        lvs_worst = res["inference"]["lvs"]["worst"]
        improvement = lvs_worst - erm_worst

        summary_data[dataset_name]["separation_scores"].append(sep)
        summary_data[dataset_name]["erm_worst"].append(erm_worst)
        summary_data[dataset_name]["lvs_worst"].append(lvs_worst)
        summary_data[dataset_name]["improvements"].append(improvement)

        print(
            f"{corr_level:<8} {sep:<12.4f} {erm_worst:<12.1f} {lvs_worst:<12.1f} {improvement:+<12.1f}"
        )

experiment_data["summary"] = summary_data

# Plotting
fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 3, figsize=(15, 12))
datasets_list = list(dataset_creators.keys())

for col, dataset_name in enumerate(datasets_list):
    # Plot 1: Separation scores vs correlation
    seps = summary_data[dataset_name]["separation_scores"]
    axes[0, col].plot(correlation_levels, seps, "bo-", linewidth=2, markersize=8)

    # Plot 2: Worst-group accuracy comparison

# Additional plot: Velocity profiles across correlation levels

for row, dataset_name in enumerate(datasets_list):

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")
    experiment_data = {}

datasets_list = ["colored_mnist", "colored_fashion_mnist", "colored_cifar10"]
correlation_levels = [0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99]

# Plot 1: Summary ablation plot (Separation, Worst-Group Accuracy, Improvement)
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 3, figsize=(15, 12))
    summary = experiment_data.get("summary", {})

    for col, dataset_name in enumerate(datasets_list):

        seps = summary[dataset_name]["separation_scores"]

        )

        )
        axes[1, col].set_xlabel("Spurious Correlation")

        )

        axes[2, col].set_ylabel("LVS Improvement (%)")
        axes[2, col].set_title(f"{dataset_name}\nLVS Improvement over ERM")

# Plot 4: Group-wise accuracy comparison (ERM vs LVS) for correlation 0.9
try:
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 5))
    corr_key = "corr_0.9"

    for col, dataset_name in enumerate(datasets_list):
        if corr_key in experiment_data and dataset_name in experiment_data[corr_key]:
            data = experiment_data[corr_key][dataset_name]

            group_names = list(erm_groups.keys())

try:

    plt.suptitle(

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "loss_curves_by_correlation.png"),
        dpi=150,
        bbox_inches="tight",
    )
    plt.close()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error creating loss curves plot: {e}")
    plt.close()

print("All plots saved successfully.")

The adaptive methods will include percentile-based (e.g., 25th percentile) and standard deviation-based (mean - 1*std) cutoffs on the plateau epoch feature. code:
import os
import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.optim as optim
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, TensorDataset
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy import stats
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")
os.makedirs(working_dir, exist_ok=True)

device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
print(f"Using device: {device}")


def create_colored_mnist(n_samples=10000, spurious_corr=0.9):
    from torchvision import datasets

    mnist = datasets.MNIST(root="./data", train=True, download=True)
    images = mnist.data.numpy()[:n_samples]
    labels = mnist.targets.numpy()[:n_samples]
    binary_labels = (labels >= 5).astype(np.int64)
    colored_images = np.zeros((n_samples, 3, 28, 28), dtype=np.float32)
    spurious_labels = np.zeros(n_samples, dtype=np.int64)
    for i in range(n_samples):
        img = images[i].astype(np.float32) / 255.0
        aligned = np.random.random() < spurious_corr
        color_idx = binary_labels[i] if aligned else 1 - binary_labels[i]
        spurious_labels[i] = color_idx
        colored_images[i, color_idx] = img
    return colored_images, binary_labels, spurious_labels


def create_colored_fashion_mnist(n_samples=10000, spurious_corr=0.9):
    from datasets import load_dataset

    dataset = load_dataset("fashion_mnist", split="train")
    images = np.array([np.array(img) for img in dataset["image"][:n_samples]])
    labels = np.array(dataset["label"][:n_samples])
    tops = [0, 2, 3, 4, 6]
    binary_labels = np.array([0 if l in tops else 1 for l in labels], dtype=np.int64)
    colored_images = np.zeros((n_samples, 3, 28, 28), dtype=np.float32)
    spurious_labels = np.zeros(n_samples, dtype=np.int64)
    for i in range(n_samples):
        img = images[i].astype(np.float32) / 255.0
        aligned = np.random.random() < spurious_corr
        color_idx = binary_labels[i] if aligned else 1 - binary_labels[i]
        spurious_labels[i] = color_idx
        colored_images[i, 0 if color_idx == 0 else 2] = img
    return colored_images, binary_labels, spurious_labels


def create_colored_cifar10(n_samples=10000, spurious_corr=0.9):
    from datasets import load_dataset

    dataset = load_dataset("cifar10", split="train")
    animals = [2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7]
    binary_labels = np.array(
        [
            0 if dataset[i]["label"] in animals else 1
            for i in range(min(n_samples, len(dataset)))
        ],
        dtype=np.int64,
    )
    colored_images = np.zeros((len(binary_labels), 3, 32, 32), dtype=np.float32)
    spurious_labels = np.zeros(len(binary_labels), dtype=np.int64)
    for idx in range(len(binary_labels)):
        img = np.array(dataset[idx]["img"]).astype(np.float32) / 255.0
        img = img.transpose(2, 0, 1)
        aligned = np.random.random() < spurious_corr
        color_idx = binary_labels[idx] if aligned else 1 - binary_labels[idx]
        spurious_labels[idx] = color_idx
        colored_images[idx] = img
        bw = 4
        channel = 0 if color_idx == 0 else 2
        colored_images[idx, channel, :bw, :] = 1.0
        colored_images[idx, channel, -bw:, :] = 1.0
        colored_images[idx, channel, :, :bw] = 1.0
        colored_images[idx, channel, :, -bw:] = 1.0
    return colored_images, binary_labels, spurious_labels


class ImprovedCNN(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_size=28, dropout_rate=0.3, n_hidden=128):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(3, 32, 3, padding=1)
        self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(32)
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1)
        self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(64)
        self.pool = nn.MaxPool2d(2, 2)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout_rate)
        self.relu = nn.ReLU()
        final_size = input_size // 4
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(64 * final_size * final_size, n_hidden)
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(n_hidden, 2)
        self.n_hidden = n_hidden

    def forward(self, x, return_features=False, neuron_weights=None):
        x = self.pool(self.relu(self.bn1(self.conv1(x))))
        x = self.pool(self.relu(self.bn2(self.conv2(x))))
        x = x.view(x.size(0), -1)
        features = self.relu(self.fc1(x))
        if neuron_weights is not None:
            features = features * neuron_weights.to(features.device)
        x = self.dropout(features)
        x = self.fc2(x)
        return (x, features) if return_features else x


def cohens_d(group1, group2):
    n1, n2 = len(group1), len(group2)
    if n1 < 2 or n2 < 2:
        return 0.0
    pooled_std = np.sqrt(
        ((n1 - 1) * np.var(group1) + (n2 - 1) * np.var(group2)) / (n1 + n2 - 2)
    )
    return (np.mean(group1) - np.mean(group2)) / (pooled_std + 1e-8)


def extract_velocity_features(grad_history):
    """Extract velocity features from gradient history"""
    n_epochs, n_neurons = grad_history.shape
    features = np.zeros((n_neurons, 4))
    for i in range(n_neurons):
        features[i, 0] = grad_history[:5, i].mean()
        features[i, 1] = grad_history[-10:, i].mean()
        decay_rate = (features[i, 0] - features[i, 1]) / (features[i, 0] + 1e-8)
        features[i, 2] = decay_rate
        threshold = 0.1 * grad_history[:, i].max()
        plateau_idx = np.where(grad_history[:, i] < threshold)[0]
        features[i, 3] = plateau_idx[0] if len(plateau_idx) > 0 else n_epochs
    return features


def cluster_neurons_kmeans(grad_history, n_clusters=2):
    """Fixed K-Means clustering approach (baseline)"""
    features = extract_velocity_features(grad_history)
    features_norm = (features - features.mean(axis=0)) / (features.std(axis=0) + 1e-8)
    kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=n_clusters, random_state=42, n_init=10)
    labels = kmeans.fit_predict(features_norm)
    cluster_plateau_means = [
        features_norm[labels == c, 3].mean() for c in range(n_clusters)
    ]
    spurious_cluster = np.argmin(cluster_plateau_means)
    spurious_neurons = labels == spurious_cluster
    return spurious_neurons, ~spurious_neurons, features[:, 3]


def cluster_neurons_percentile(grad_history, percentile=25):
    """Adaptive percentile-based threshold approach"""
    features = extract_velocity_features(grad_history)
    plateau_epochs = features[:, 3]
    threshold = np.percentile(plateau_epochs, percentile)
    spurious_neurons = plateau_epochs <= threshold
    return spurious_neurons, ~spurious_neurons, plateau_epochs


def cluster_neurons_stddev(grad_history, n_std=1.0):
    """Adaptive standard deviation-based threshold approach"""
    features = extract_velocity_features(grad_history)
    plateau_epochs = features[:, 3]
    threshold = np.mean(plateau_epochs) - n_std * np.std(plateau_epochs)
    spurious_neurons = plateau_epochs <= threshold
    return spurious_neurons, ~spurious_neurons, plateau_epochs


def cluster_neurons_median(grad_history):
    """Adaptive median-based threshold approach"""
    features = extract_velocity_features(grad_history)
    plateau_epochs = features[:, 3]
    threshold = np.median(plateau_epochs)
    spurious_neurons = plateau_epochs <= threshold
    return spurious_neurons, ~spurious_neurons, plateau_epochs


def evaluate_with_groups(model, loader, y_true, s_true, neuron_weights=None):
    model.eval()
    all_preds = []
    with torch.no_grad():
        for batch_x, _ in loader:
            batch_x = batch_x.to(device)
            outputs = model(batch_x, neuron_weights=neuron_weights)
            _, preds = outputs.max(1)
            all_preds.extend(preds.cpu().numpy())
    all_preds = np.array(all_preds)
    groups = {}
    for y in [0, 1]:
        for s in [0, 1]:
            mask = (y_true == y) & (s_true == s)
            if mask.sum() > 0:
                groups[(y, s)] = (all_preds[mask] == y_true[mask]).mean() * 100
    avg_acc = (all_preds == y_true).mean() * 100
    worst_group = min(groups.values()) if groups else 0
    return avg_acc, worst_group, groups


def compute_separation_metrics(
    fc1_grads, spurious_neurons, core_neurons, plateau_epochs
):
    """Compute separation metrics between spurious and core neurons"""
    separation_metrics = {
        "plateau_epoch": abs(
            cohens_d(plateau_epochs[spurious_neurons], plateau_epochs[core_neurons])
        ),
        "initial_velocity": abs(
            cohens_d(
                fc1_grads[:5].mean(axis=0)[spurious_neurons],
                fc1_grads[:5].mean(axis=0)[core_neurons],
            )
        ),
        "final_velocity": abs(
            cohens_d(
                fc1_grads[-10:].mean(axis=0)[spurious_neurons],
                fc1_grads[-10:].mean(axis=0)[core_neurons],
            )
        ),
    }
    return separation_metrics


def train_model_with_tracking(
    config, train_loader, test_loader, test_y, test_s, input_size=28
):
    """Train model and track velocity data"""
    torch.manual_seed(config.get("seed", 42))
    np.random.seed(config.get("seed", 42))
    model = ImprovedCNN(input_size=input_size, dropout_rate=config["dropout"]).to(
        device
    )
    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    optimizer = optim.AdamW(
        model.parameters(), lr=config["lr"], weight_decay=config["weight_decay"]
    )
    scheduler = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
        optimizer, T_max=config["n_epochs"]
    )

    velocity_data = {"fc1_grad": []}
    results = {
        "metrics": {"train": [], "val": []},
        "losses": {"train": [], "val": []},
        "worst_group": [],
    }
    best_val_loss, patience_counter, best_model_state = float("inf"), 0, None

    for epoch in range(config["n_epochs"]):
        model.train()
        train_loss, correct, total = 0.0, 0, 0
        epoch_grads = []

        for batch_x, batch_y in train_loader:
            batch_x, batch_y = batch_x.to(device), batch_y.to(device)
            optimizer.zero_grad()
            outputs = model(batch_x)
            loss = criterion(outputs, batch_y)
            loss.backward()
            torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)
            epoch_grads.append(model.fc1.weight.grad.norm(dim=1).cpu().numpy())
            optimizer.step()
            train_loss += loss.item()
            _, predicted = outputs.max(1)
            total += batch_y.size(0)
            correct += predicted.eq(batch_y).sum().item()

        scheduler.step()
        velocity_data["fc1_grad"].append(np.mean(epoch_grads, axis=0))
        train_acc = 100.0 * correct / total
        avg_train_loss = train_loss / len(train_loader)

        model.eval()
        val_loss, correct, total = 0.0, 0, 0
        with torch.no_grad():
            for batch_x, batch_y in test_loader:
                batch_x, batch_y = batch_x.to(device), batch_y.to(device)
                outputs = model(batch_x)
                loss = criterion(outputs, batch_y)
                val_loss += loss.item()
                _, predicted = outputs.max(1)
                total += batch_y.size(0)
                correct += predicted.eq(batch_y).sum().item()

        val_acc = 100.0 * correct / total
        avg_val_loss = val_loss / len(test_loader)
        avg_acc, worst_group, _ = evaluate_with_groups(
            model, test_loader, test_y, test_s
        )

        results["losses"]["train"].append(avg_train_loss)
        results["losses"]["val"].append(avg_val_loss)
        results["metrics"]["train"].append(train_acc)
        results["metrics"]["val"].append(val_acc)
        results["worst_group"].append(worst_group)

        if avg_val_loss < best_val_loss:
            best_val_loss = avg_val_loss
            best_model_state = {
                k: v.cpu().clone() for k, v in model.state_dict().items()
            }
            patience_counter = 0
        else:
            patience_counter += 1

        if epoch % 10 == 0:
            print(f"  Epoch {epoch}: val={val_acc:.1f}%, worst={worst_group:.1f}%")

        if patience_counter >= config["patience"]:
            print(f"  Early stopping at epoch {epoch}")
            break

    if best_model_state:
        model.load_state_dict({k: v.to(device) for k, v in best_model_state.items()})

    return model, velocity_data, results


def run_ablation_study(model, fc1_grads, test_loader, test_y, test_s, config):
    """Run ablation comparing different clustering methods"""
    methods = {
        "kmeans": lambda g: cluster_neurons_kmeans(g, n_clusters=2),
        "percentile_25": lambda g: cluster_neurons_percentile(g, percentile=25),
        "percentile_33": lambda g: cluster_neurons_percentile(g, percentile=33),
        "stddev_1": lambda g: cluster_neurons_stddev(g, n_std=1.0),
        "stddev_0.5": lambda g: cluster_neurons_stddev(g, n_std=0.5),
        "median": lambda g: cluster_neurons_median(g),
    }

    ablation_results = {}
    weight_factor = config.get("reweight_factor", 0.3)

    # Get ERM baseline (no reweighting)
    avg_acc_erm, worst_erm, groups_erm = evaluate_with_groups(
        model, test_loader, test_y, test_s
    )

    for method_name, method_fn in methods.items():
        spurious_neurons, core_neurons, plateau_epochs = method_fn(fc1_grads)

        # Compute separation metrics
        if spurious_neurons.sum() > 0 and core_neurons.sum() > 0:
            sep_metrics = compute_separation_metrics(
                fc1_grads, spurious_neurons, core_neurons, plateau_epochs
            )
            velocity_signature_separation = np.mean(list(sep_metrics.values()))
        else:
            sep_metrics = {
                "plateau_epoch": 0,
                "initial_velocity": 0,
                "final_velocity": 0,
            }
            velocity_signature_separation = 0

        # Apply LVS reweighting
        neuron_weights = torch.ones(model.n_hidden)
        neuron_weights[spurious_neurons] = weight_factor

        avg_acc_lvs, worst_lvs, groups_lvs = evaluate_with_groups(
            model, test_loader, test_y, test_s, neuron_weights
        )

        ablation_results[method_name] = {
            "n_spurious": int(spurious_neurons.sum()),
            "n_core": int(core_neurons.sum()),
            "spurious_ratio": float(spurious_neurons.mean()),
            "separation_metrics": sep_metrics,
            "velocity_signature_separation": velocity_signature_separation,
            "erm": {"avg": avg_acc_erm, "worst": worst_erm},
            "lvs": {"avg": avg_acc_lvs, "worst": worst_lvs},
            "improvement": worst_lvs - worst_erm,
            "plateau_epochs": plateau_epochs,
            "spurious_neurons": spurious_neurons,
        }

    return ablation_results


# Main execution
print("Creating datasets...")
torch.manual_seed(42)
np.random.seed(42)
datasets_info = {}

print("Loading ColoredMNIST...")
X_train_cm, y_train_cm, s_train_cm = create_colored_mnist(
    n_samples=10000, spurious_corr=0.95
)
X_test_cm, y_test_cm, s_test_cm = create_colored_mnist(
    n_samples=2000, spurious_corr=0.5
)
mean_cm, std_cm = X_train_cm.mean(), X_train_cm.std() + 1e-8
datasets_info["colored_mnist"] = {
    "train": TensorDataset(
        torch.FloatTensor((X_train_cm - mean_cm) / std_cm), torch.LongTensor(y_train_cm)
    ),
    "test": TensorDataset(
        torch.FloatTensor((X_test_cm - mean_cm) / std_cm), torch.LongTensor(y_test_cm)
    ),
    "test_y": y_test_cm,
    "test_s": s_test_cm,
    "input_size": 28,
}

print("Loading ColoredFashionMNIST...")
X_train_fm, y_train_fm, s_train_fm = create_colored_fashion_mnist(
    n_samples=10000, spurious_corr=0.95
)
X_test_fm, y_test_fm, s_test_fm = create_colored_fashion_mnist(
    n_samples=2000, spurious_corr=0.5
)
mean_fm, std_fm = X_train_fm.mean(), X_train_fm.std() + 1e-8
datasets_info["colored_fashion_mnist"] = {
    "train": TensorDataset(
        torch.FloatTensor((X_train_fm - mean_fm) / std_fm), torch.LongTensor(y_train_fm)
    ),
    "test": TensorDataset(
        torch.FloatTensor((X_test_fm - mean_fm) / std_fm), torch.LongTensor(y_test_fm)
    ),
    "test_y": y_test_fm,
    "test_s": s_test_fm,
    "input_size": 28,
}

print("Loading ColoredCIFAR10...")
X_train_cf, y_train_cf, s_train_cf = create_colored_cifar10(
    n_samples=10000, spurious_corr=0.95
)
X_test_cf, y_test_cf, s_test_cf = create_colored_cifar10(
    n_samples=2000, spurious_corr=0.5
)
mean_cf, std_cf = X_train_cf.mean(), X_train_cf.std() + 1e-8
datasets_info["colored_cifar10"] = {
    "train": TensorDataset(
        torch.FloatTensor((X_train_cf - mean_cf) / std_cf), torch.LongTensor(y_train_cf)
    ),
    "test": TensorDataset(
        torch.FloatTensor((X_test_cf - mean_cf) / std_cf), torch.LongTensor(y_test_cf)
    ),
    "test_y": y_test_cf,
    "test_s": s_test_cf,
    "input_size": 32,
}

config = {
    "lr": 0.001,
    "batch_size": 128,
    "n_epochs": 60,
    "dropout": 0.4,
    "weight_decay": 5e-4,
    "seed": 42,
    "patience": 15,
    "reweight_factor": 0.3,
}

experiment_data = {}
for dataset_name, data_info in datasets_info.items():
    print(f"\n{'='*50}\nDataset: {dataset_name}\n{'='*50}")
    train_loader = DataLoader(
        data_info["train"], batch_size=config["batch_size"], shuffle=True
    )
    test_loader = DataLoader(
        data_info["test"], batch_size=config["batch_size"], shuffle=False
    )

    # Train model
    model, velocity_data, training_results = train_model_with_tracking(
        config,
        train_loader,
        test_loader,
        data_info["test_y"],
        data_info["test_s"],
        data_info["input_size"],
    )

    fc1_grads = np.array(velocity_data["fc1_grad"])

    # Run ablation study
    ablation_results = run_ablation_study(
        model, fc1_grads, test_loader, data_info["test_y"], data_info["test_s"], config
    )

    experiment_data[dataset_name] = {
        "training": training_results,
        "velocity_data": velocity_data,
        "ablation": ablation_results,
    }

    print(f"\nAblation Results for {dataset_name}:")
    print(
        f"{'Method':<15} {'N_Spur':<8} {'Ratio':<8} {'Sep':<8} {'ERM_W':<8} {'LVS_W':<8} {'Improv':<8}"
    )
    print("-" * 70)
    for method, res in ablation_results.items():
        print(
            f"{method:<15} {res['n_spurious']:<8} {res['spurious_ratio']:.3f}    {res['velocity_signature_separation']:.3f}    {res['erm']['worst']:.1f}%    {res['lvs']['worst']:.1f}%    {res['improvement']:+.1f}%"
        )

# Summary
print("\n" + "=" * 80)
print("ABLATION STUDY SUMMARY: K-Means vs Adaptive Threshold Methods")
print("=" * 80)

for ds in datasets_info:
    print(f"\n{ds}:")
    ablation = experiment_data[ds]["ablation"]
    best_method = max(ablation.keys(), key=lambda m: ablation[m]["improvement"])
    print(
        f"  Best method: {best_method} (improvement: {ablation[best_method]['improvement']:+.1f}%)"
    )
    print(f"  K-Means improvement: {ablation['kmeans']['improvement']:+.1f}%")
    print(
        f"  K-Means separation: {ablation['kmeans']['velocity_signature_separation']:.4f}"
    )

# Plotting
fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 4, figsize=(20, 12))
methods_to_plot = ["kmeans", "percentile_25", "stddev_1", "median"]
colors = {
    "kmeans": "blue",
    "percentile_25": "red",
    "stddev_1": "green",
    "median": "orange",
}

for row, ds in enumerate(datasets_info.keys()):
    ablation = experiment_data[ds]["ablation"]
    fc1_grads = np.array(experiment_data[ds]["velocity_data"]["fc1_grad"])

    # Plot 1: Separation scores comparison
    methods = list(ablation.keys())
    separations = [ablation[m]["velocity_signature_separation"] for m in methods]
    axes[row, 0].bar(
        range(len(methods)),
        separations,
        color=["blue", "red", "coral", "green", "lightgreen", "orange"],
    )
    axes[row, 0].set_xticks(range(len(methods)))
    axes[row, 0].set_xticklabels(methods, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
    axes[row, 0].set_title(f"{ds}\nSeparation Scores")
    axes[row, 0].set_ylabel("Cohen's d (avg)")

    # Plot 2: Worst-group accuracy comparison
    erm_worst = [ablation[m]["erm"]["worst"] for m in methods]
    lvs_worst = [ablation[m]["lvs"]["worst"] for m in methods]
    x = np.arange(len(methods))
    axes[row, 1].bar(x - 0.2, erm_worst, 0.4, label="ERM", alpha=0.7)
    axes[row, 1].bar(x + 0.2, lvs_worst, 0.4, label="LVS", alpha=0.7)
    axes[row, 1].set_xticks(x)
    axes[row, 1].set_xticklabels(methods, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
    axes[row, 1].set_title(f"{ds}\nWorst-Group Accuracy")
    axes[row, 1].legend()

    # Plot 3: Velocity signatures for K-Means
    spurious_km = ablation["kmeans"]["spurious_neurons"]
    axes[row, 2].plot(
        fc1_grads[:, spurious_km].mean(axis=1),
        label="Spurious (KMeans)",
        color="red",
        alpha=0.8,
    )
    axes[row, 2].plot(
        fc1_grads[:, ~spurious_km].mean(axis=1),
        label="Core (KMeans)",
        color="blue",
        alpha=0.8,
    )
    axes[row, 2].set_title(f"{ds}\nVelocity (K-Means)")
    axes[row, 2].legend()
    axes[row, 2].set_xlabel("Epoch")

    # Plot 4: Plateau epoch distribution with thresholds
    plateau_epochs = ablation["kmeans"]["plateau_epochs"]
    axes[row, 3].hist(
        plateau_epochs, bins=20, alpha=0.7, color="gray", label="All neurons"
    )
    axes[row, 3].axvline(
        np.percentile(plateau_epochs, 25), color="red", linestyle="--", label="P25"
    )
    axes[row, 3].axvline(
        np.mean(plateau_epochs) - np.std(plateau_epochs),
        color="green",
        linestyle="--",
        label="Mean-1std",
    )
    axes[row, 3].axvline(
        np.median(plateau_epochs), color="orange", linestyle="--", label="Median"
    )
    axes[row, 3].set_title(f"{ds}\nPlateau Epoch Distribution")
    axes[row, 3].legend(fontsize=8)
    axes[row, 3].set_xlabel("Plateau Epoch")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(os.path.join(working_dir, "ablation_clustering_comparison.png"), dpi=150)
plt.close()


# Convert numpy arrays to lists for serialization
def convert_to_serializable(obj):
    if isinstance(obj, np.ndarray):
        return obj.tolist()
    elif isinstance(obj, dict):
        return {k: convert_to_serializable(v) for k, v in obj.items()}
    elif isinstance(obj, list):
        return [convert_to_serializable(i) for i in obj]
    elif isinstance(obj, (np.int64, np.int32)):
        return int(obj)
    elif isinstance(obj, (np.float64, np.float32)):
        return float(obj)
    else:
        return obj


experiment_data_serializable = convert_to_serializable(experiment_data)
np.save(
    os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"),
    experiment_data_serializable,
    allow_pickle=True,
)
print(f"\nResults saved to {working_dir}")

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import os

working_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "working")

try:
    experiment_data = np.load(
        os.path.join(working_dir, "experiment_data.npy"), allow_pickle=True
    ).item()
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error loading experiment data: {e}")
    experiment_data = None

if experiment_data:
    datasets = list(experiment_data.keys())

    # Plot 1: Training and Validation Loss/Accuracy curves for each dataset
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 2, figsize=(14, 12))
        for idx, ds in enumerate(datasets):
            train_loss = experiment_data[ds]["training"]["losses"]["train"]
            val_loss = experiment_data[ds]["training"]["losses"]["val"]
            train_acc = experiment_data[ds]["training"]["metrics"]["train"]
            val_acc = experiment_data[ds]["training"]["metrics"]["val"]
            worst_group = experiment_data[ds]["training"]["worst_group"]
            epochs = range(len(train_loss))

            axes[idx, 0].plot(epochs, train_loss, label="Train Loss", color="blue")
            axes[idx, 0].plot(epochs, val_loss, label="Val Loss", color="orange")
            axes[idx, 0].set_title(f"{ds}\nTraining/Validation Loss")
            axes[idx, 0].set_xlabel("Epoch")
            axes[idx, 0].set_ylabel("Loss")
            axes[idx, 0].legend()

            axes[idx, 1].plot(epochs, train_acc, label="Train Acc", color="blue")
            axes[idx, 1].plot(epochs, val_acc, label="Val Acc", color="orange")
            axes[idx, 1].plot(
                epochs,
                worst_group,
                label="Worst Group Acc",
                color="red",
                linestyle="--",
            )
            axes[idx, 1].set_title(f"{ds}\nAccuracy Curves")
            axes[idx, 1].set_xlabel("Epoch")
            axes[idx, 1].set_ylabel("Accuracy (%)")
            axes[idx, 1].legend()

        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "training_validation_curves_all_datasets.png"),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating training curves plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 2: Ablation - Separation scores and improvements comparison
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(18, 10))
        for idx, ds in enumerate(datasets):
            ablation = experiment_data[ds]["ablation"]
            methods = list(ablation.keys())
            separations = [
                ablation[m]["velocity_signature_separation"] for m in methods
            ]
            improvements = [ablation[m]["improvement"] for m in methods]
            colors = ["blue", "red", "coral", "green", "lightgreen", "orange"]

            axes[0, idx].bar(range(len(methods)), separations, color=colors)
            axes[0, idx].set_xticks(range(len(methods)))
            axes[0, idx].set_xticklabels(methods, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
            axes[0, idx].set_title(f"{ds}\nVelocity Signature Separation (Cohen's d)")
            axes[0, idx].set_ylabel("Separation Score")

            axes[1, idx].bar(range(len(methods)), improvements, color=colors)
            axes[1, idx].axhline(y=0, color="black", linestyle="-", linewidth=0.5)
            axes[1, idx].set_xticks(range(len(methods)))
            axes[1, idx].set_xticklabels(methods, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
            axes[1, idx].set_title(
                f"{ds}\nWorst-Group Accuracy Improvement (LVS - ERM)"
            )
            axes[1, idx].set_ylabel("Improvement (%)")

        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(
                working_dir, "ablation_separation_improvement_all_datasets.png"
            ),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating ablation separation plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 3: ERM vs LVS worst-group accuracy comparison
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
        for idx, ds in enumerate(datasets):
            ablation = experiment_data[ds]["ablation"]
            methods = list(ablation.keys())
            erm_worst = [ablation[m]["erm"]["worst"] for m in methods]
            lvs_worst = [ablation[m]["lvs"]["worst"] for m in methods]
            x = np.arange(len(methods))

            axes[idx].bar(
                x - 0.2, erm_worst, 0.4, label="ERM", alpha=0.7, color="steelblue"
            )
            axes[idx].bar(
                x + 0.2, lvs_worst, 0.4, label="LVS", alpha=0.7, color="coral"
            )
            axes[idx].set_xticks(x)
            axes[idx].set_xticklabels(methods, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
            axes[idx].set_title(f"{ds}\nWorst-Group Accuracy: ERM vs LVS")
            axes[idx].set_ylabel("Accuracy (%)")
            axes[idx].legend()

        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "erm_vs_lvs_worst_group_all_datasets.png"),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating ERM vs LVS plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 4: Velocity signatures (spurious vs core neurons) using K-Means
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
        for idx, ds in enumerate(datasets):
            fc1_grads = np.array(experiment_data[ds]["velocity_data"]["fc1_grad"])
            spurious_neurons = np.array(
                experiment_data[ds]["ablation"]["kmeans"]["spurious_neurons"]
            )

            axes[idx].plot(
                fc1_grads[:, spurious_neurons].mean(axis=1),
                label="Spurious Neurons",
                color="red",
                linewidth=2,
            )
            axes[idx].plot(
                fc1_grads[:, ~spurious_neurons].mean(axis=1),
                label="Core Neurons",
                color="blue",
                linewidth=2,
            )
            axes[idx].fill_between(
                range(len(fc1_grads)),
                fc1_grads[:, spurious_neurons].mean(axis=1),
                alpha=0.2,
                color="red",
            )
            axes[idx].fill_between(
                range(len(fc1_grads)),
                fc1_grads[:, ~spurious_neurons].mean(axis=1),
                alpha=0.2,
                color="blue",
            )
            axes[idx].set_title(f"{ds}\nVelocity Signatures (K-Means Clustering)")
            axes[idx].set_xlabel("Epoch")
            axes[idx].set_ylabel("Mean Gradient Norm")
            axes[idx].legend()

        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "velocity_signatures_kmeans_all_datasets.png"),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating velocity signatures plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    # Plot 5: Plateau epoch distribution with threshold markers
    try:
        fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
        for idx, ds in enumerate(datasets):
            plateau_epochs = np.array(
                experiment_data[ds]["ablation"]["kmeans"]["plateau_epochs"]
            )

            axes[idx].hist(
                plateau_epochs,
                bins=20,
                alpha=0.7,
                color="gray",
                edgecolor="black",
                label="All neurons",
            )
            axes[idx].axvline(
                np.percentile(plateau_epochs, 25),
                color="red",
                linestyle="--",
                linewidth=2,
                label="Percentile 25",
            )
            axes[idx].axvline(
                np.mean(plateau_epochs) - np.std(plateau_epochs),
                color="green",
                linestyle="--",
                linewidth=2,
                label="Mean - 1 Std",
            )
            axes[idx].axvline(
                np.median(plateau_epochs),
                color="orange",
                linestyle="--",
                linewidth=2,
                label="Median",
            )
            axes[idx].set_title(
                f"{ds}\nPlateau Epoch Distribution with Adaptive Thresholds"
            )
            axes[idx].set_xlabel("Plateau Epoch")
            axes[idx].set_ylabel("Count")
            axes[idx].legend(fontsize=9)

        plt.tight_layout()
        plt.savefig(
            os.path.join(working_dir, "plateau_epoch_distribution_all_datasets.png"),
            dpi=150,
        )
        plt.close()
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error creating plateau distribution plot: {e}")
        plt.close()

    print("All plots saved successfully.")
plot_plan: Looking at the experiment code, I need to visualize the ablation study comparing K-Means vs adaptive threshold clustering methods for neuron classification across three datasets (ColoredMNIST, ColoredFashionMNIST, ColoredCIFAR10). The experiment_data contains training metrics (losses, accuracies, worst-group accuracy), velocity data (fc1 gradients over epochs), and ablation results for different clustering methods (kmeans, percentile_25, percentile_33, stddev_1, stddev_0.5, median). I'll create plots for: (1) training/validation curves per dataset, (2) velocity signature separation comparison across methods, (3) worst-group accuracy improvements, (4) plateau epoch distributions with thresholds, and (5) velocity signatures comparing spurious vs core neurons.

plot_analyses.analysis:
Across datasets, the optimization behavior shows a consistent pattern: train loss rapidly decays to near-zero while validation loss remains substantially higher and often noisy, indicating strong reliance on shortcut/spurious signal and overfitting to the training correlation.

colored_mnist (loss/acc curves): Train loss → ~0 by ~10 epochs and train acc saturates ~99–100%. Validation accuracy rises to ~90–93% and then plateaus; worst-group accuracy (WGA) climbs quickly from very low early values to ~80–86% but remains well below val acc and fluctuates. The widening generalization gap (train vs val loss) plus persistent WGA gap suggests the model achieves high average performance while still failing on the minority/worst group—classic spurious correlation behavior.

colored_fashion_mnist: Similar but less severe. Train acc ~99–100% quickly; val acc ~96–98% with small oscillations. WGA stabilizes high (~92–96%) with modest fluctuations, indicating spurious signal exists but core features are also learned sufficiently; the shortcut is less dominating than in colored_mnist.

colored_cifar10: Most problematic. Train acc ~96–99% while val acc stays ~70–83% and validation loss is high and even trends upward later, signaling overfitting and/or miscalibration. WGA is very low early and remains much lower than val acc, ending roughly in the mid-50s to low-60s with large variance. This dataset exhibits the largest robustness gap and is the key stress test for LVS interventions.

Velocity/plateau diagnostics and thresholding ablations (multi-panel figures):
- Separation scores (Cohen’s d) are consistently highest for K-means clustering of velocity signatures across all three datasets (~3.2–4.3+), while heuristic plateau-threshold rules (percentile_25/33, median, mean-std variants) yield lower but still sizable separation (~1.8–3.1). This indicates the velocity trajectories contain strong signal to distinguish two neuron populations.
- Velocity curves (K-means) show the purported signature clearly: the ‘spurious’ cluster (red) has a sharp early drop to near-zero velocity within ~1–3 epochs and stays near-zero; the ‘core’ cluster (blue) decays more gradually and remains non-trivial for many epochs. This matches the hypothesis (fast learn + early plateau for spurious).
- Plateau epoch distributions are highly skewed: a large mass at very early epochs (0–2) plus a long tail to later epochs (10–26 depending on dataset). The adaptive thresholds (P25, median, mean-1std) all fall in the early region, meaning these rules will classify many early-plateau neurons as “spurious.” This is plausible, but it also risks conflating “easy but legitimate” features with spurious ones unless coupled with additional evidence.

Crucial outcome: worst-group accuracy improvements are essentially zero.
- Bar plots comparing ERM vs LVS across thresholding methods show LVS ≈ ERM for all datasets and all threshold rules.
- The explicit “Improvement (LVS - ERM)” plots are ~0 everywhere, with only a tiny positive bump for colored_mnist under one setting (stddev_0.5) on the order of <0.5 percentage points; colored_fashion_mnist and colored_cifar10 show no visible gain.

Implications for the ablation-study goals:
1) Detection signal is strong, but the downstream reweighting/inference intervention is not translating into robustness gains. This suggests either (a) identified neurons are not causally responsible for the spurious reliance, (b) the reweighting factor is too weak/incorrectly applied, (c) spurious information is distributed/entangled so suppressing a subset of neurons doesn’t change decision behavior, or (d) the evaluation is not isolating the intended intervention (e.g., reweighting applied at wrong layer/time).
2) K-means appears best for separation; however, since WGA is unchanged, “better separation” is not currently predictive of “better robustness.” You likely need an ablation that correlates separation score with WGA change across many runs/thresholds to see if there is any monotonic relationship.
3) colored_cifar10 remains the critical failure case: despite strong separation (Cohen’s d ~2.8–4.3), WGA does not improve, implying that either the spurious feature is not localized in the monitored neurons/layer or that suppressing them harms both groups similarly.

Concrete next experiments suggested by these plots:
- Causal validation of detected neurons: perform neuron ablation/zeroing at inference for the “spurious” cluster vs random neurons matched by norm, and measure WGA/avg acc deltas. If random ablation matches LVS ablation, the detector is not identifying causal shortcut units.
- Layer-wise LVS: compute velocity signatures across multiple layers (early conv blocks vs penultimate) and test which layer’s detected set yields the largest WGA gain when suppressed.
- Stronger interventions: instead of static reweighting, try gradient surgery during training (downweight gradients flowing through spurious cluster after its plateau) or add a penalty encouraging continued learning on slow/core units.
- Threshold sensitivity sweep: since adaptive thresholds cluster near epoch 0–2, explicitly sweep plateau cutoffs (e.g., 0,1,2,3,5,10) and neuron fractions (top 5/10/20/40%) and plot WGA vs fraction to detect any non-monotonic “sweet spot.”
- colored_cifar10-specific: check if WGA is bottlenecked by representation capacity (val acc is low) rather than shortcut reliance alone; consider stronger backbone, longer training with regularization, or data augmentation, then re-run LVS to see if robustness becomes intervention-sensitive.

Overall: the plots provide strong evidence that LVS-style velocity signatures exist and are separable, but they do not yet support the claim that velocity-based neuron weighting improves worst-group accuracy—at least under the current implementation/settings.

vlm_feedback_summary: Received multiple plots covering (i) train/val loss and accuracy with worst-group accuracy for colored_mnist, colored_fashion_mnist, colored_cifar10; (ii) separation scores (Cohen’s d) for several thresholding/clustering rules; (iii) velocity trajectories for spurious vs core clusters; (iv) plateau epoch histograms with adaptive thresholds; and (v) ERM vs LVS worst-group accuracy and improvement bars. Main visual takeaway: velocity signatures are clearly separable (especially with K-means), but LVS yields ~0 worst-group accuracy improvement over ERM across datasets, with at most a tiny gain in one colored_mnist setting.
ablation_name: Adaptive Threshold-Based vs Fixed K-Means Clustering for Neuron Classification

Ablation name: Neuron Reweighting vs Neuron Pruning for Spurious Feature Mitigation.
I'll implement an ablation study comparing three approaches for handling spurious neurons: (1) soft reweighting (the baseline LVS method with factor 0.3), (2) hard pruning (completely zeroing out all spurious neurons), and (3) partial pruning (zeroing out only the top-k% most spurious neurons based on plateau epoch). The study will evaluate each method across all three datasets, tracking worst-group accuracy and average accuracy to understand whether spurious neurons contain any useful information worth preserving.
